tool.name: Kraken Segment
tool.description: segment text regions and lines in document images
tool.command: #set $native_out_ext = 'json' @SET_OUTPUT_EXTENSION@ @REJECT_MULTIPAGE_TIFF_BASED_ON_FILE_METADATA@ @CREATE_SYMLINKS@ #if $segmenter.type == 'baseline' and $segmenter.model_source.source == "history" #set $model_name = str($segmenter.model_source.model.name) ln -s '$segmenter.model_source.model' '$model_name' && #end if #if $segmenter.type == 'boxes' #if $image.ext == 'pdf' echo "The legacy box segmenter requires a binarized image input. PDF input seems to be not supported with the legacy box segmenter since its fails even for 1-bit bilevel PDFs. Please extract the PDF pages to binarized PNG/TIFF images first, or use the neural baseline segmenter." >&2; exit 1; #else colors=\$(identify -format "%k" 'input.${image.ext}') && if [ "\$colors" -gt 2 ]; then echo "The legacy box segmenter requires a binarized image, but there were \$colors colors detected. Please run Kraken Binarize first or use the neural baseline segmenter." >&2; exit 1; fi && #end if #end if @RUN_KRAKEN@ segment #if $segmenter.type == 'baseline' --baseline #if $segmenter.model_source.source == "datatable" --model '${segmenter.model_source.model}' #elif $segmenter.model_source.source == "history" --model '$model_name' #end if #else --boxes #if str($segmenter.scale).strip() --scale '$segmenter.scale' #end if --maxcolseps '$segmenter.maxcolseps' $segmenter.colseps $segmenter.hlines --pad '$segmenter.pad' #end if --text-direction '$text_direction' --input-pad '$input_pad'

param.name: output_format_flag
param.label: Output format
param.option: abbyyXML (cannot be used as input for kraken ocr)
hOCR (cannot be used as input for kraken ocr)
JSON (cannot be used as input for kraken ocr)

param.name: type
param.label: Segmenter type
param.option: Legacy box segmenter (requires binarized input image)

expand.label: Segmentation model
expand.help: Select a segmentation model to use. The default is kraken's internal stock model

param.label: Layout analysis scale
param.help: Increase this to help with larger text structures; decrease it to adapt better to smaller text. Very low values may miss or fragment lines.

param.label: Maximum column separators
param.help: Maximum number of column separators to detect during segmentation. Increase it for layouts with more text columns; decrease it for simpler layouts or single-column pages.

param.name: colseps
param.label: Column separator color
param.help: Select `White` for separators formed by blank page background; select `Black` for layouts with printed vertical rules or dark divider lines.

param.name: hlines
param.label: Horizontal ruling lines
param.help: Select `Remove horizontal lines` to ignore lines such as table rules, form lines, or ruled-paper lines; select `Keep horizontal lines` if these lines should remain part of the segmentation input.

param.label: Segmented regions padding
param.help: Increase this to add more margin around each segmented region; decrease it to keep region boundaries tighter.

param.label: Text direction
param.help: Select `Horizontal, left-to-right` text for English, German, or Greek; `Horizontal, right-to-left` text for Arabic or Hebrew; vertical text with columns ordered left-to-right for some Mongolian layouts; or vertical text with columns ordered right-to-left for traditional Chinese, Japanese, or Korean layouts.

param.label: Input image padding
param.help: Increase it to add more margin around the document before layout analysis; decrease it to keep the input closer to its original boundaries.

has_text.text: The legacy box segmenter requires a binarized image
PDF input seems to be not supported with the legacy box segmenter

tool.help:
**What it does**
kraken is an open-source, turn-key automatic text recognition (ATR) system optimized for historical and non-Latin script writing, This tool only exposes Kraken's segmentation command.
Segmentation is the process of detecting text regions and lines in document images, which is a prerequisite for the OCR step. Output can be in various formats, such as [ALTO](https://www.loc.gov/standards/alto/), [PageXML](https://github.com/PRImA-Research-Lab/PAGE-XML/wiki), [hOCR](https://kba.github.io/hocr-spec/1.2/) and [abbyyXML](https://support.abbyy.com/hc/en-us/articles/360017270080-Output-XML-document).
It is recommended to use the neural baseline segmenter with Kraken's internal stock segmentation model or you can select an alternative model. Next to it, kraken's legacy box segmenter is also available. However, it (a) expects binarized input and (b) does not support PDF input.
**Important**
The JSON output of the kraken segment command cannot be used as the input for the Kraken OCR command.
**License**
Kraken is licensed under the Apache-2.0 license.
